sglang.title: [XPU][CPU] Enable the native path of DeepSeek
sglang.author: airMeng
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently the native path of deepseek v3 is broken, so fix the broken native path which will benefit CPU and all other accelerators
Has verified on CPU and XPU:
TRITON_AVAILABLE=0 python3 -m sglang.bench_one_batch --batch-size 1 --input 32 --output 32 --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 --tru

before

ops.impl("cutlass_scaled_mm_supports_block_fp8",
           &cutlass_scaled_mm_supports_fp8);

after

ops.impl("cutlass_scaled_mm_supports_bl

Closes #14767

vllm.title: [Model] Mamba2 Prefill Performance Tweaks: Fixing Flurry of Unnecessary Memory Copies
vllm.author: cyang49
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We found an issue while profiling vLLM running Bamba-9B model inference.
Before:

As can be seen in the Nsight Systems trace, per Mamba layer there are 2 phases where frequent memory copies happen. They are not necessary, or can be fused to reduce the number of copies. This PR fixes these issues.
Af

vllm.title: [V1] Fix model parameterization for structured output tests
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The previous method ran the test with model_name set to a list, and the
LLM class would fail because we passed in that list. This change fixes
it to parameterize the tests with the two models to be tested.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: Update to torch==2.6.0
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Only updates for CUDA. Successfully built locally on H100 CUDA 12.5 system and tested with vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
We should upgrade other hardware backends separately. For instance, CPU is blocked by IPEX in the Dockerfile.cpu
FIX #12719

vllm.title: [Build/CI] Upgrade aiohttp to incldue CVE fix
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The previous version is vulnerable to the following:

GHSA-27mf-ghqm-j3j8

It does not appear that vLLM is affected. However, we should avoid
explicitly requiring a version of a library with known CVEs, or we will
get flagged for it.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [Neuron][CI] update docker run command
vllm.author: liangfu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There are two changes:

change network from host to bridge

This is because we want to enable EnableDockerUserNamespaceRemap=true, which enables Docker user namespace remapping so docker runs as buildkite-agent. Otherwise, docker would raise Error response from daemon: cannot share the host's networ

vllm.title: [Bugfix][V1] Fix flashinfer sampling
vllm.author: DefTruth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
small fix base on #14788
Currently, we have errors when using FlashInfer>=v0.2.3 for top-p & top-k sampling. As a workaround, we disable FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling by default while FlashInfer>=v0.2.3. The sampling API removes the success return value of all sampling API, which is not comp

vllm.title: Disable outlines cache by default
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
GHSA-mgrm-fgjv-mhv8
Outlines provides a cache for its compiled grammars on the local
filesystem. This cache has been on by default in vLLM. Outlines is also
available by default through the OpenAI compatible API server.
A malicious user can send a stream of very short decoding requests with
unique s

vllm.title: [V1] V1 Enablement Oracle
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

adds oracle for V1 feature enablement
removes usage of envs.VLLM_USE_V1 from code base (now only used in tests + in oracle)
enables V1 by default

BEHAVIOR
# * If VLLM_USE_V1 is unset, we enable V1 for "supported features"
#   and fall back to V0 for experimental or unsupported features.
#

vllm.title: [Attention] Get rid of mla cache alignment
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
With FlashMLA now being the default on Nvidia GPU and the fact that it seemingly doesnt help the Triton backend anymore/ever-did (bit of a mystery). I think we can go ahead and rip this out reclaiming the memory lost to padding:
VLLM_MLA_CUDA_MEM_ALIGN_KV_CACHE=1 VLLM_USE_V1=0 VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SA

vllm.title: [Build/CI] Upgrade jinja2 to get 3 moderate CVE fixes
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Versions prior to 3.1.6 are vulnerable to these 3 CVEs. We should ensure
we are using >=3.1.6 to avoid any potential security vulnerability via
jinja2.

GHSA-gmj6-6f8f-6699
GHSA-q2x7-8rv6-6q7h
GHSA-cpwx-vrp4-4pq7

Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: [Bugfix] EAGLE output norm bug
vllm.author: luyuzhe111
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The current dummy output norm for EAGLE is incorrect. After fixing, the accepted tokens improved drastically from 1.4 to 1.8 with 2 speculated tokens. Essentially, the dummy RMS norm should return hidden + residual instead of hidden only.
Additionally, I enabled tracking the number of accepted token
